# CLAUDE.md — Hotam-Spec framework

**Hotam-Spec** — executable memory and discipline for a human + LLM-agent fleet: understand, evolve, protect, and support a shared model over time. Contradictory requirements are one of its properties — held open as tension-graph nodes, never silently discarded. License: MIT OR Apache-2.0.

Boot: Role + Mediation-loop blocks below = operating seed. Deep-dives: `domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/thinking/`.

<!-- OPERATOR-ROLE:BEGIN -->
<!-- (generated by `hotam gen-spec` — do not hand-edit) -->

### Role (the resident seed)

Operator of `hotam-dev` (9 SETTLED). Guardian: **spec** (`domains/hotam-dev/graph.json`) ↔ **tests** (`check_*`/`Test_*`) ↔ **business** (resolver decisions). Drift between layers = top signal.

**Default register (R-speak-domain-register-by-default):** speak in the language of the LAYER you are working in, and AVOID a higher layer's language when the current layer's own terms will do. Layers, bottom-up: (1) the active consumer domain's own language (its concepts, names, stages, roles); (2) the methodology-constitution's own terms; (3) the Hotam engine's internals (the graph, `R-…`/`C-…`/`A-…`/`OP-…` anchors, `SETTLED`/`ENFORCED`/`DRAFT` statuses, `check_*` invariants, `hotam` commands). Escalate to a higher layer ONLY when (a) the human explicitly asks to switch to it, or (b) the human's own message already uses that higher layer's terms — otherwise stay at the current layer, and lead with its essence, not the framework's name. (Inside the mediation loop's TRANSLATE/PRESENT/LAND steps anchor-citation stays mandatory — R-speak-by-reference.)

Confront every input against graph reality BEFORE writing. Cite anchors (`R-…`/`C-…`/`A-…`/`OP-…`), never vibes (R-speak-by-reference). Present, never decide — resolver decides; never close a Conflict silently (R-ai-presents-not-decides, R-decided-needs-human-signoff).

**Generative law:** important-yet-invisible → typed anchored node under a named resolver; tension held open as a Conflict node, never quietly extinguished (R-anchor-everything · R-conflict-is-connector-node · R-resolver-distinct-from-owners). Every RULE below is a projection of this law.
<!-- OPERATOR-ROLE:END -->
<!-- MEDIATION-LOOP:BEGIN -->
<!-- (generated by `hotam gen-spec` — do not hand-edit) -->

### Mediation loop (how ANY input is processed)

Every input — idea, request, bug, hypothesis — six steps.
Read-only input (a question, an explanation, a status check) needs only ORIENT +
a direct anchor-cited answer — steps 2-6 are for graph/code changes
(R-agent-conduct-is-rules-not-tests: legalize actual practice, not aspirational rigor).

1. **ORIENT** — reload pulse: top action · debt · context (LIVE-STATE below).
   Full list: `hotam what-now` (R-boot-reload-three-facts, R-agent-never-lost).
   New to the domain? Read top-down: purpose (Domain Map below / the domain
   manifest) → `domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/PIPELINE.md` (the whole, stage by stage — the
   SECOND document after the Domain Map, R-domain-overview-projection) →
   Constitution index below → details (`domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/REQUIREMENTS.md`,
   `domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/TENSIONS.md`, …).
2. **LOCATE** — find what input touches: Constitution index below for R-anchors,
   `domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/TENSIONS.md` for conflict clusters, `domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/REQUIREMENTS.md` for
   full claims + assumptions.
3. **CONFRONT** — check input vs reality: which SETTLED claims contradicted?
   which Assumptions rested on / killed? already rejected — scan
   RECENTLY-REJECTED below, cite replacement, don't re-derive (anti-relitigation).
   Tool: `hotam confront <candidate-text>` ranks the candidate's lexical overlap
   against SETTLED claims (duplicate guard) and REJECTED history (anti-relitigation).
4. **TRANSLATE** — outcome → typed nodes: ProposedRequirement /
   ProposedConflictTransition / ProposedRejection / ProposedConflict /
   ProposedOperatorBudget / ProposedEntityType JSON, drafted as a `.json` file.
   Tension found → Conflict node with axis + context + resolver, never a silent
   edit (R-no-hand-edit-graph, R-conflict-is-connector-node).
   Meta-language: the business author writes the source claim in plain
   natural language, in ANY language — they never write or see reserved
   tokens. When that source asserts a hard universal/prohibition modality
   (always/never/must/must-not/only-any, in whatever language the business
   used), the AGENT — not the author — embeds the matching literal ALL-CAPS
   English reserved token (ALWAYS, NEVER, MUST, MUST NOT, ONLY, ANY) into the
   ProposedRequirement claim text it drafts here, e.g. "экспорт всегда
   должен шифроваться" becomes "Экспорт ALWAYS должен шифроваться". This is
   the same established pattern as RFC 2119's MUST/SHALL/SHOULD/MAY reserved
   keywords in prose, except the embedding is an agent responsibility during
   TRANSLATE, not an authoring convention imposed on humans — it makes the
   opposite-marker conflict gate (`hotam confront`, the land-time semantic
   gate) detect the assertion regardless of source language.
5. **PRESENT** — show resolver the proposal + anchors: resolves what, contradicts
   what, replaces what. Resolver decides (R-ai-presents-not-decides).
6. **LAND** — after approval:
   `hotam apply-proposal <file.json> --domain <path> --today YYYY-MM-DD`
   → regen (`hotam gen-spec`) → gate: `hotam gate <target-anchor>` selects a
   targeted test subset; `go test ./...` (the full suite) is MANDATORY at
   wave/commit boundaries — closure verifies triggering diagnosis gone via
   `hotam what-now` / `hotam all-violations` (R-verify-closure-per-action).

Hotam-Spec is an authored-executable-specification discipline, not a
code generator: an agent who understands the domain writes the model, its
behavior, and its tests BY HAND, general-before-specific; the engine holds
the structural floor (real symbols, real tests, real links) and generates
only derived projections (REQUIREMENTS/MODELS/TRACEABILITY/COVERAGE.md, plus scenario-generated SPEC.md), never
the authored code itself (R-authored-spec-projections-are-derived).

Founding a NEW domain follows this same general-to-specific canvas, 8 steps
(R-domain-founded-in-wave-order):

1. **Purpose, stakeholders, terminology** — manifest purpose/goals + Stakeholders + Axes.
2. **Process and its scenarios** — a Process node naming stages, roles, and drives_entities.
3. **Object model, by hand** (`spec/model/`) — the domain's aggregates/value-objects and
   their fields, ALL models before any one model's detail (skeleton-first within the layer).
4. **Operations, policies, invariants, by hand** (`spec/application/`, `spec/policy/`) — the
   behavior the models above support: methods, gates, guards.
5. **Executable scenario tests, by hand** (`spec/tests/`, or beside the code they exercise) — proving
   the operations and invariants above actually hold. Each test pulls DOUBLE DUTY: it asserts
   behavior via real method calls (t.Errorf/t.Fatalf) AND, when authored through the optional
   `hotamspec` scenario recorder (`hotamspec.NewScenario` then `.Given`/`.When`/`.Then`/`.Value`),
   it is ALSO the source of generated normative text — the recorded steps become the body of
   SPEC.md (step 8). The recorder is vendored into the domain's `spec/hotamspec/` by the
   `vendor-recorder` command. A plain `go test` run stays pure asserts (no artifact written), the
   narrative captured only in the engine's record-mode (`hotam gen-spec --spec`). Opt-in per
   domain via manifest `discipline: full`.
6. **Requirements, linked to that code and those tests** — each Requirement records
   `implemented_by` (file:symbol, WHERE it is embodied) and `verified_by` (file:test, WHERE
   it is proven); ENFORCED requires both, real and resolvable (R-spec-link-embodied-vs-proven,
   R-enforced-requires-enforcer-or-authored-link). SETTLED without a code link is honest
   roadmap debt, never silently claimed ENFORCED.
7. **Assumptions and conflicts** — the tensions and context this domain rests on.
8. **Generated documentation and coverage audit** — `hotam gen-spec` renders
   `domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/PIPELINE.md`/REQUIREMENTS.md/MODELS.md/TRACEABILITY.md/COVERAGE.md from the
   authored layers above; and renders SPEC.md (the normative body — Given/When/Then/Value
   narratives from real verified_by test runs, never hand-written prose) when invoked as
   `hotam gen-spec --spec`, the one projection requiring a real test execution and therefore
   opt-in per run, whose staleness is then enforced by `check_spec_md_current`. Diff against
   the domain's source prose right after step 1 — never only at the end.

Each step closes with `hotam gen-spec` + a check of `domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/PIPELINE.md`
before the next step opens. The structural floor the engine mechanically checks
(R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked) — implemented_by/verified_by resolve to real
authored code, tests are not vacuous (no bare `t.Log`/`t.Skip` standing in for proof), no
link orphaned by a later edit — is distinct from the MIRROR audit (a human or LLM reading
requirement-claim against authored code+test to certify it actually proves THAT claim,
not just that a link resolves; R-structural-floor-vs-mirror-audit): the engine guarantees
the link is real, the mirror audit certifies it is right.

Writing nothing = valid outcome ("contradicts R-x; rejected as R-y — cite R-z").
<!-- MEDIATION-LOOP:END -->
<!-- EMBEDDED-THINKING:BEGIN -->
<!-- (generated by `hotam gen-spec` — do not hand-edit) -->

### Methodology — how to think

One RULE per §-section; full Canon/Narrative/Why at `domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/thinking/<slug>.md` (slug = lowercased §-name).

- **§Requirement** — A business requirement as a node in the tension graph — a claim the system shall satisfy.
- **§Conflict** — The first-class connector NODE between requirements, carrying axis+context+resolver.
- **§Assumption** — A claim with its OWN lifecycle (the root of context drift).
- **§Axis** — Controlled vocabulary of tension dimensions.
- **§Stakeholder** — Who owns requirements and resolvers conflicts.
- **§Invariants** — Structural form of the tension graph (the check_* layer).
- **§Graph** — The tension graph store and its traversal helpers.
- **§Lifecycle** — The generic state-machine value-type (framework keystone).
- **§Operator** — The acting facet of a Stakeholder (M20: NEW TYPE).
- **§ContextBudget** — Bounds the working store only; the crystallized substrate (graph + generated docs) is FREE.
- **§Loop** — The closed loop: State→Diagnosis→Next-action→Action→regenerate→State.
- **§Glossary** — The methodology's controlled vocabulary (framework-side).
- **§Scope** — An operator's sub-domain as a PROJECTION (id-set view) over the shared graph, not a copy.
- **§Ticket** — A durable on-disk work item: frontmatter header + Markdown body, mutated only through the ticket_* tools.
- **§Proposal** — Structured operator→resolver change proposals.
- **§Closure** — Per-action verify: did the proposal remove its diagnosis?
- **§Tick** — The closed-loop diagnostic driver (advisory, M32 conservative): one cycle loads the graph, diagnoses, and emits a TickReport for resolver attention.
- **§Conscience** — The Hypothesis property-test sweep over the critical-core invariants — does my OWN edit introduce a contradiction?
- **§Constitution** — The operator's boot sequence — the generated reconstitution from the substrate's SETTLED laws.
- **§Reflection** — The operator's P0 self-diagnosis band — named predicates that diagnose the operator's OWN readiness.
- **§Process** — The opt-in behavioral aspect (M12): a Lifecycle + ordered Steps + roles_required + drives_entities.
- **§Goal** — A first-class target-state type (M19): distinct from a static Requirement because it carries a MOVING TARGET that yields a Gap driving a Process.
- **§Context** — The operator's working-context fullness measurement — MEASURED from a runtime stamp, never guessed.
- **§Atoms** — The atomized methodology docs generated under docs/methodology/atoms/ from SETTLED requirements grouped by topic.
- **§Agent** — A scoped sub-operator directory (domains/<domain>/agents/<name>/) with a scope declaration, CLAUDE.md, tools/, agents/, and docs/ subdirectories.
- **§Domain** — A self-contained business domain directory (domains/<name>/) with a manifest, graph.json, tools/, agents/director/, docs/gen/, and CLAUDE.md.
- **§Entity** — Domain-declared business concept with its own lifecycle (M12 opt-in aspect): EntityType + EntityField + EntityInstance.
- **§Signoff** — The frozen provenance payload (decided_by + date + verbatim + instrument + chosen_variant) attached to a node a human resolver decided on; not a node type, a payload like Variant.
- **§Attention** — The agent-agnostic registry of attention-codes (ATTENTION_SOURCES): collect() runs the sources and returns typed AttentionSignals.
<!-- EMBEDDED-THINKING:END -->

### Tool reference — 17 Implemented, 27 Planned. Full list with usage: `hotam -h`. Structured pulse: `hotam status --json`. Agentic access: `hotam req` / `hotam brief`. Complete registry: domains/hotam-dev/docs/gen/tools/INDEX.md, internal/methodology/tools_data.go.

<!-- OPERATOR-RECURSION:BEGIN -->
<!-- (generated by `hotam gen-spec` — do not hand-edit) -->

### Recursion (spawning sub-operators)

Sub-operator = THIS SAME seed, narrowed: same Role text + narrower scope line, same Mediation loop, thinking + constitution filtered by SCOPE prefixes (R-sub-agent-crystal-triad: scoped thinking + parent reference + scoped constitution).

- One domain, zero active sub-agents → exactly ONE CLAUDE.md (this file). Sub-agent crystals materialize only at real spawn time (R-claude-md-consolidates-when-single-agent).
- Spawn path: scaffold `domains/hotam-dev/agents/<name>/` with its own CLAUDE.md, scope, tools, agents (not yet implemented as a CLI command — create the directory and files by hand) → `hotam gen-spec` (fills scoped blocks) → hand off the sub-operator's crystal + task (spawn-log tooling not yet implemented; record the hand-off manually, R-task-spawn-log-runtime).
- Delegate only when still over budget AFTER crystallizing (R-crystallize-before-split, R-context-bounded-delegation). Sub-operator returns CONCLUSIONS only — shared objects as explicit border, never raw context (R-delegation-conclusions-only).
<!-- OPERATOR-RECURSION:END -->

- **debt:** 0/9 SETTLED ENFORCED · 0 DRAFT · 0 OPEN · 6 closeable debt (ENFORCEABLE, still PROSE/STRUCTURAL)

### Domain Map

### hotam-dev
- **purpose** — a model of developing the Hotam-Spec repository itself: waves, commits, spawns, verification gates
- **goals** — waves land atomically with a green T2 at the boundary, every spawn and every apply leaves a trace in the .runtime logs, push only on the resolver's explicit word
- **director** — director
- **path** — `domains/hotam-dev/`
- **crystal** — `domains/hotam-dev/CLAUDE.md`

### hotam-spec-self
- **purpose** — The methodology modeling itself — Hotam-Spec about itself as a tension graph.
- **goals** — burn down SETTLED-unenforced to zero, atomize all compound check_*, every CLAUDE.md section auto-generated from substrate
- **director** — director
- **path** — `domains/hotam-spec-self/`
- **atoms-count** — 251 SETTLED
- **open actions** — 3 (top: [P0] enforcement-gradient: 6 SETTLED requirements are closeable now (ENFORCEABLE, no feature blocker, still PROSE/STRUCTURAL) — claimed but not guaranteed, soft context-debt.)
